question: Define Project Scope.
answer_a: It is the description of the required work that is necessary to complete the project.
answer_b: It is the description of the required work and resources that are needed to complete the project.
answer_c: It is the design of experiments that are used to complete the project work.
answer_d: It is the combination of the cost and the schedule which is required to complete the project work.

question: Which of the following comprises the Project Life Cycle?

Suppose you are the project manager on a thermal power plant project. How will you manage your team members, if there is a difference of opinion over the design of turbine?
answer_a: Organize meetings to resolve the misunderstanding and agree on a common solution.
answer_b: Copy the design of turbine from a similar past project.
answer_c: Defer the decision to procure turbine components as much as possible.
answer_d: Procure the turbine parts from the nearest located vendor.

question: You find that activity in your project is very risky. How will you estimate the duration of this activity when your team member has given the optimistic, most likely and pessimistic duration for the activity?

What is the role of the change control board?
answer_b: Doing performance appraisal for team members involved in implementing changes.
answer_c: Encouraging team members to raise more change requests.
answer_d: Assessing the impact of the change on Project objectives.

question: Being the project manager on a STP project, you decide to respond to an identified risk by contracting out work. Which of the following will lead to the minimum risk of the buyer?

Suppose you are assigned as the project manager of a 30 months project which is in the executing phase. What will be the best way to resolve a conflict among the technical experts in your team?
answer_a: Remove this technical work from the scope of project work.
answer_b: Find the root cause of the disagreement.
answer_c: Resolve the conflict in favor of the senior most technical expert.
answer_d: Hire an external consultant to enforce his decision.

question: What should be your primary concern while adding resources to your project?
answer_a: Effect of adding resources on Cost and Schedule.
answer_b: Effect of adding resources on project end date.
answer_c: Effect of adding resources on S curve.
answer_d: Effect of adding resources on RAM.

question: According to PMBOK, project management processes are organized into which of the following order of process groups?
answer_a: Initiating, designing, planning, executing, controlling, and closing
answer_c: Initiating, planning, executing, monitoring and controlling, and closing
answer_d: Initiating, planning, executing, monitoring, and closing

question: Which of the following statements is true regarding negative float?
answer_a: The project manager should try to remove negative float by reducing scope.
answer_b: The project manager should try to remove negative float by crashing or fast- tracking.
answer_c: It is desirable to have the negative float in the project.
answer_d: There cannot be any negative float in the project.

Schedule Variance is:
answer_a: A positive value means that the project will likely be delayed.
answer_b: A negative value means that the project will likely be delayed.

question: You are currently working as a Project Manager in Realty Logistics. You have been given responsibility for moving Infrastructure of ABC Software LTD to a new location. For that, you need to identify the stakeholders of your project. Based on the Power/Interest Grid that you have created, which of the following types of stakeholders’ expectation should be managed closely?
What is the company-wide policy which mandates all project quality known as?
Which one of the following describes the difference between a Standard and a Deviation accurately?
answer_a: Regulations are mandatory but standards may be seen as guidelines.
answer_b: Standards are optional but regulations are not.
answer_c: Standards are mandatory but regulations are not.
answer_d: Regulations and Standards are essentially the same.

question: What will be the BAC of your project, if it has a CPI of 0.80 and an EAC of $ 1265825?

answer_c: Manual Forecasting of the cost of remaining work

question: How will you assess a situation where six observations are within the UPPER CONTROL LIMIT and control line while the seventh observation is observed within UPPER CONTROL LIMIT and Control line?

question: Which of the following can be used for Trend Analysis?

Which of the following is the objective of conducting Bidder Conferences?
answer_a: To ensure that all parties get answers to their questions.
answer_b: To make sure that potential sellers are treated as per the type of questions asked.
answer_c: To make sure that all questions from potential sellers are answered privately.
answer_d: To make sure that all potential sellers are treated equally and have access to the same information.

question: What is the Japanese method of modern quality management called, which relies on continuous small improvements involving everyone from the top management to the lowest level worker in the organization?
Herzberg divided motivation factors into two classes: satisfiers and dissatisfies. Which of the following are the examples of satisfiers then?
answer_a: Vacation time, assignment of a personal staff assistant. PMP Math Questions For the first three questions, assume you are managing the following project: You are the project manager of a project that is budgeted to cost $1 million at completion. The project will last ten months and its budget is spread evenly across each month. It is currently 50% complete and has so far cost $750K but only produced value of $500K.
answer_b: The sense of personal achievement, work satisfaction.
answer_d: Plush office space, performance-based salary raise.
